summary: The presented case describes recurrent, sudden anger spikes triggered by minor events, with high bodily arousal, surprise at the response, post-episode shame, and a perceived identity-anger mismatch. The eight changeable levers below span biological, psychological, and social domains (and their interactions) to reduce episode frequency and intensity, improve arousal regulation, decrease trigger sensitivity, align behavior with self-identity, and bolster readiness to change. Each lever is a modifiable predictor (a lever) with a clear mechanism, measurable targets, and feasible implementation pathways, structured to map onto the observed criteria (C01–C08). Time horizons range 6–12 weeks, with consideration for safety and privacy where relevant.

solutions.predictor_variable: Emotion Regulation Skillset: Cognitive reactivity reduction and impulse-control training (CBT-based)
solutions.solution_candidate: 6-week CBT-based anger regulation program with weekly coaching and daily home practice, including cognitive restructuring (reframing triggering thoughts), time-out strategies, and practice in impulse-control rehearsals.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthening cognitive reappraisal and inhibitory control reduces the frequency of sudden anger spikes, dampens peak bodily arousal, lowers sensitivity to minor triggers, reduces the surprise of reactions, decreases post-episode shame, and improves alignment between behavior and self-identity. This also enhances readiness to change by increasing perceived self-efficacy.
solutions.measurement: Weekly counts of anger episodes; peak intensity on 0–10 scale; trigger severity rating; post-episode shame 0–10; self-identity conflict 0–10; adherence to practice (days practiced).
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns identified; monitor for increased distress during cognitive work; provide access to clinician if worsening mood.
solutions.mapping_query: Connects with all criteria (C01–C08); leverages CBT-based regulation to reduce episode frequency, arousal, trigger impact, unexpectedness, shame, identity conflict, and inhibition capacity.

solutions.predictor_variable: Mindfulness-based somatic regulation (breathing, body awareness) to downregulate autonomic arousal during episodes
solutions.solution_candidate: 8-week mindfulness-based stress reduction program focusing on paced breathing, body scanning, and non-judgmental awareness; optional wearable feedback for respiration and heart-rate variability (HRV) tracking.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Mindfulness enhances autonomic regulation, reduces intense bodily sensations during triggers, increases nonreactivity to triggers, and supports inhibitory control over outward expressions. This can decrease peak arousal, reduce unexpectedness, and support identity-aligned responses.
solutions.measurement: Episode intensity (0–10) during episodes; frequency per week; HRV or breathing rate during triggers (if available); self-reported nonreactivity; adherence to practice.
solutions.safety_notes: Privacy and data handling for wearables; ensure user comfort with monitoring; have opt-out options.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C02, C03, C04, C01, and C07 via increased arousal regulation and nonreactivity during triggers.

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep health optimization to stabilize mood and autonomic regulation
solutions.solution_candidate: 6–8 week sleep health program including consistent bedtimes, wake times, caffeine management, and sleep-conducive environment; monitor sleep quality and daytime mood.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep stabilizes mood and reduces baseline emotional reactivity, lowering peak bodily arousal (C02), decreasing trigger sensitivity (C03), and mitigating post-episode shame by improving affect regulation and cognitive clarity (C05).
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/quality (PSQI or daily diary); episodes per week; peak intensity; morning mood.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for sleep disorder symptoms; consult clinician if insomnia persists.
solutions.mapping_query: Relates to C02–C05 by stabilizing arousal and affect; sleep quality mediates reactivity and post-episode affect.

solutions.predictor_variable: Graded exposure to low-severity triggers with inhibitory learning (exposure-based)
solutions.solution_candidate: 12-week structured exposure program to systematically re-encounter common triggers (e.g., driving, being cut off) with coping strategies and response prevention to reduce reactive anger.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Repeated, controlled exposure with coping strategies discourages automatic anger responses, weakens associative strength between minor triggers and rage, reduces surprise, and promotes healthier coping behaviors that align with self-identity.
solutions.measurement: Trigger-provoked episodes per week; peak intensity during exposure tasks; self-reported urge to act; diary-based progress notes.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure gradual escalation; clinician guidance recommended; monitor for distress during exposure.
solutions.mapping_query: Directly targets C03 (trigger sensitivity) and C01 (episode frequency) with inhibitory learning; also impacts C02 and C04.

solutions.predictor_variable: Self-compassion and values-based identity integration to reduce mismatch and shame
solutions.solution_candidate: 8-week program combining self-compassion training with values clarification and identity repair work; journaling, compassionate self-talk, and identity-alignment exercises; optional peer support.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhancing self-compassion reduces self-criticism after episodes (C05) and helps integrate angry reactions into a values-consistent self-image (C06); greater value-consistent action increases motivation to change (C08) and supports adaptive reactivity (C04).
solutions.measurement: Post-episode shame (0–10); identity conflict (0–10); readiness to change (0–10); frequency and variability of episodes.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for avoidance or rumination; ensure supportive context.
solutions.mapping_query: Addresses C05, C06, and C08 primarily; supports C04 via improved self-regulation and value-based action.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured social support and accountability networks
solutions.solution_candidate: 6-week group-based support with weekly check-ins, accountability partners, and family involvement; peer coaching to practice regulation strategies.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced belonging and accountability reduce isolation, increase motivation to change, and provide real-world reinforcement for self-regulation during triggers, thereby reducing episode frequency and bolstering inhibitory control.
solutions.measurement: Group attendance; perceived social support (scales); episodes per week; readiness to change.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure confidentiality; obtain consent for family involvement; manage group dynamics.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C01 and C08 via social support and accountability; potentially improves C07 by providing safer contexts for emotion expression.

solutions.predictor_variable: Aerobic/cardiorespiratory fitness to improve autonomic regulation
solutions.solution_candidate: 12-week supervised cardio program (e.g., walking, cycling) progressing to 150 minutes/week with HR monitoring and gradual intensity increases.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular aerobic activity improves autonomic balance and mood regulation, reducing peak arousal during episodes, lowering trigger reactivity, and enhancing inhibitory control over outward expressions.
solutions.measurement: Weekly exercise minutes; resting HR; HRV indices (if available); episodes per week; peak intensity.
solutions.safety_notes: Medical clearance recommended if cardiometabolic risk; tailor intensity to fitness level.
solutions.mapping_query: Links to C02 (arousal) and C03 (trigger sensitivity) via improved autonomic regulation and control during episodes.

solutions.predictor_variable: Digital self-monitoring and feedback for personalized care
solutions.solution_candidate: 6–12-week digital self-monitoring with daily logs of episodes, triggers, arousal, and after-action reflections; automated feedback and coaching prompts to tailor interventions.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Systematic self-monitoring increases awareness, enables rapid adjustment of strategies, and creates data-driven personalization of interventions; supports motivation to change and identity-aligned behaviors.
solutions.measurement: Daily/weekly logs: episodes, triggers, 0–10 arousal, post-episode mood; adherence to feedback; progress slope.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure privacy and data security; obtain informed consent for data collection; allow opt-out.
solutions.mapping_query: Universal mapping to C01–C08 through data-informed personalization and feedback loops.
